title: Gsd/phase 30 admin user simulation docs 30 admin user simulation md docs 30 vigilar md

review_comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,93 @@
+"""
+Simulation Middleware - Inyecta contexto de simulación en handlers.
+
+Se aplica después de DatabaseMiddleware para tener acceso al container.
+Inyecta user_context en data para que RoleDetectionMiddleware use el rol simulado.
+"""
+import logging
+from typing import Callable, Dict, Any, Awaitable
+
+from aiogram import BaseMiddleware
+from aiogram.types import Message, CallbackQuery, TelegramObject
+
+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+
+
+class SimulationMiddleware(BaseMiddleware):
+    """
+    Middleware que resuelve e inyecta el contexto de simulación del usuario.
+
+    Uso:
+        # Aplicar DESPUÉS de DatabaseMiddleware:
+        dp.message.middleware(SimulationMiddleware())
+        dp.callback_query.middleware(SimulationMiddleware())
+
+    Inyecta en data dictionary:
+        - data["user_context"]: ResolvedUserContext (contexto resuelto con simulación)
+
+    Requiere:
+        - DatabaseMiddleware debe ejecutarse primero (inyecta container en data)
+
+    Pattern: Sigue RoleDetectionMiddleware structure (user extraction, data injection)
+    """
+
+    async def __call__(
+        self,
+        handler: Callable[[TelegramObject, Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[Any]],
+        event: TelegramObject,
+        data: Dict[str, Any]
+    ) -> Any:
+        """
+        Ejecuta el middleware.
+
+        Args:
+            handler: Handler a ejecutar después de la resolución
+            event: Evento de Telegram (Message, CallbackQuery, etc)
+            data: Data del handler (incluye container, session, etc)
+
+        Returns:
+            Resultado del handler con user_context disponible en data
+        """
+        # Extraer user del evento (siguiendo patrón RoleDetectionMiddleware)
+        user = None
+
+        if isinstance(event, Message):
+            user = event.from_user
+        elif isinstance(event, CallbackQuery):
+            user = event.from_user
+
+        if user is None:
+            # Edge case: no se pudo extraer usuario
+            logger.debug("⚠️ No se pudo extraer usuario del evento")
+            return await handler(event, data)
+
+        # Obtener container del data dictionary (inyectado por DatabaseMiddleware)
+        container = data.get("container")
+
+        logger.info(f"🎭 SimulationMiddleware executing for user {user.id}, has_container={container is not None}")
+
+        if not container:
+            # No hay container disponible (DatabaseMiddleware no ejecutado)
+            logger.warning("⚠️ No container in data, skipping context resolution")
+            return await handler(event, data)
+
+        # Resolver contexto de simulación e inyectar en data
+        try:
+            context = await container.simulation.resolve_user_context(user.id)
+            data["user_context"] = context
+            if context.is_simulating:
+                logger.info(
+                    f"🎭 Simulation active for user {user.id}: "
+                    f"mode={context.simulated_mode.value}, "
review_comments.body:
![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

Se está intentando acceder a `context.simulated_mode`, pero el dataclass `ResolvedUserContext` (definido en `bot/services/simulation.py`) no tiene este atributo. Esto provocará un `AttributeError` en tiempo de ejecución. Para solucionarlo, se debería añadir el atributo `simulated_mode: Optional[SimulationMode] = None` al dataclass `ResolvedUserContext` y asignarle el valor correspondiente en el método `SimulationStore.activate_simulation`.

@@ -59,6 +60,16 @@ def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession):
         self.session = session
         self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
 
+    def _check_simulation_block(self, user_id: int, action: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
+        """Check if user is in simulation mode and block state-changing actions.
+
+        Returns:
+            Tuple of (is_blocked, error_message)
+        """
+        if SimulationStore.is_simulating(user_id):
review_comments.body:
![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

La llamada a `SimulationStore.is_simulating(user_id)` es incorrecta. `is_simulating` es un método de instancia, pero se está llamando como un método de clase (estático). Dado que `SimulationStore` es un singleton, la llamada correcta para obtener la instancia y luego llamar al método es `SimulationStore().is_simulating(user_id)`. Este error provocará un `TypeError` en tiempo de ejecución.

```suggestion
        if SimulationStore().is_simulating(user_id):
```

review_comments.body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

Esta lógica para detectar el rol del usuario es redundante. El `RoleDetectionMiddleware` ya realiza esta detección (incluyendo la lógica de simulación) e inyecta el rol resultante como `user_role` en el diccionario `data` del handler. Para evitar la duplicación de lógica y asegurar que se utilice una única fuente de verdad, se debería obtener el rol directamente de `data['user_role']` en el handler `cmd_start` y pasarlo a esta función, en lugar de volver a calcularlo aquí.

+class SimulationMode(str, Enum):
+    """
+    Modos de simulación para admins probar comportamiento de usuarios.
+
+    Modos:
+        REAL: Operación normal, sin simulación
+        VIP: Simular usuario VIP
+        FREE: Simular usuario Free
+
+    Uso:
+        - Admins pueden activar simulación para ver el bot como otro rol
+        - Los datos reales del usuario no se modifican
+        - La simulación es temporal (TTL 30 minutos)
+    """
+
+    REAL = "real"
+    VIP = "vip"
+    FREE = "free"
+
+    def __str__(self) -> str:
+        """Retorna valor string del enum."""
+        return self.value
+
+    @property
+    def display_name(self) -> str:
+        """Retorna nombre legible del modo."""
+        names = {
+            SimulationMode.REAL: "Real",
+            SimulationMode.VIP: "Simulación VIP",
+            SimulationMode.FREE: "Simulación Free"
+        }
+        return names[self]
+
+    @property
+    def emoji(self) -> str:
+        """Retorna emoji del modo."""
+        emojis = {
+            SimulationMode.REAL: "👤",
+            SimulationMode.VIP: "⭐",
+            SimulationMode.FREE: "🆓"
+        }
+        return emojis[self]
+
+    @property
+    def simulated_role(self) -> "UserRole":
+        """Retorna el rol simulado correspondiente."""
+        from bot.database.enums import UserRole
review_comments.body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

La importación `from bot.database.enums import UserRole` es innecesaria, ya que la clase `UserRole` se define en el mismo archivo y ya está en el ámbito (scope). Se puede eliminar para simplificar el código.

review_comments.body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

El bloque de código para construir el mensaje de estado de simulación (líneas 191-213) es idéntico al que se encuentra en el handler `simulation_command` (líneas 137-161). Para mejorar la mantenibilidad y evitar la duplicación de código (principio DRY), se podría extraer esta lógica a una función auxiliar. Esta función podría aceptar `context` y `status` como argumentos y devolver el texto del mensaje formateado.

issue_comments.body:
## Summary of Changes Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request delivers a robust Admin User Simulation system, enabling administrators to thoroughly test user experiences across different roles without impacting live data. It encompasses the core logic for simulation, user interface controls for activation and management, and essential safety measures to block real-world transactions during testing. The changes also finalize the Telegram Alert Handler and provide updated project documentation to reflect these significant advancements. ### Highlights * **Admin User Simulation System**: Introduced a comprehensive system allowing administrators to simulate different user roles (VIP, Free) for testing purposes without affecting real user data. * **Middleware Integration**: Implemented a new `SimulationMiddleware` to inject the resolved user context into handler data, ensuring consistent role detection across the application, and updated `RoleDetectionMiddleware` to respect this context. * **Safety Restrictions**: Added critical safety checks to `WalletService`, `ShopService`, and `RewardService` to prevent state-changing operations (e.g., earning/spending currency, purchasing items, claiming rewards) while in simulation mode. * **Admin UI Controls**: Developed a `/simulate` command with an interactive inline keyboard for administrators to activate, deactivate, and switch between simulation modes, along with a visual banner indicating active simulation. * **Roadmap and Project State Updates**: Updated the project roadmap (`ROADMAP.md`) and current state (`STATE.md`) to reflect the completion of Phase 29 (Telegram Alert Handler) and Phase 30 (Admin User Simulation), including detailed progress and gap closures. * **Gap Closure for Simulation Integration**: Addressed integration issues by correctly registering `SimulationMiddleware` in `main.py` and modifying the `/start` handler to properly utilize the simulated user context, ensuring admins see user menus when simulating. 🧠 **New Feature in Public Preview:** You can now enable **Memory** to help **Gemini Code Assist** learn from your team's feedback. This makes future code reviews more consistent and personalized to your project's style. **Click [here](https://codeassist.google/code-review/login) to enable Memory in your admin console.**
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. Invoking Gemini You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either `/gemini ` or `@gemini-code-assist `. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with :thumbsup: and :thumbsdown: on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for GitHub and other Google products, sign up [here](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_2cyuGuTWsEw84yG).
[^1]: Review the [Privacy Notices](https://policies.google.com/privacy), [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy), [Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms), and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and [use code with caution](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/13505487).

issue_comments.body:
### Code review

Found 3 issues:

1. **AttributeError: `context.simulated_mode` does not exist** (CLAUDE.md requires type hints and valid attribute access)

`bot/middlewares/simulation.py` line 81 uses `context.simulated_mode.value` but `ResolvedUserContext` has no `simulated_mode` attribute - it should be `simulated_role`. This causes an `AttributeError` when an admin activates simulation mode.

https://github.com/carlostoek/adminpro/blob/0d84db65611e77374a6b677692e106cc152fef16/bot/middlewares/simulation.py#L79-L83

2. **TypeError: `SimulationStore.is_simulating()` called without instance** (CLAUDE.md requires correct async/await and method invocation patterns)

`bot/services/wallet.py:69`, `bot/services/shop.py:280`, and `bot/services/reward.py:656` call `SimulationStore.is_simulating(user_id)` as a class method, but `is_simulating` is an **instance method** requiring `self`. This causes `TypeError: is_simulating() missing 1 required positional argument: 'admin_id'` when users attempt state-changing operations (earn/spend besitos, purchases, reward claims) during simulation.

https://github.com/carlostoek/adminpro/blob/0d84db65611e77374a6b677692e106cc152fef16/bot/services/wallet.py#L63-L71

3. **Simulation context lost during deep link token activation** (bug due to missing parameter propagation)

`bot/handlers/user/start.py` line 217 does not pass `user_context` when calling `_send_welcome_message` after deep link token activation. The `_activate_token_from_deeplink` function also lacks `user_context` as a parameter. This causes the simulation context to be lost when an admin simulates a user role and uses a deep link, falling back to real role detection.

https://github.com/carlostoek/adminpro/blob/0d84db65611e77374a6b677692e106cc152fef16/bot/handlers/user/start.py#L210-L220
